meaning: vi.玩戏法,诓骗,篡改vt.耍弄,歪曲,篡改n.玩戏法,魔术,欺骗
examples: My uncle taught me to juggle.
It can be tough trying to juggle a career and a family.
Women very often have to juggle work with their family commitments.
Thousands of tiny children pedal about on unicycles and juggle balls in perfect synchronicity.
Goldman even manages to deftly juggle reputation risk in the course of its business.
